description:
GOAL: Port specialize_return from typer.rs (AST-shaped) to operate over fz_ir. Given a FnIr and per-parameter Descr inputs, return the inferred return-Descr. This is the kernel that .13 (interpreter <-> JIT tier-up) needs to make per-call-shape specialization decisions.

## Why this is atomic

Before: specialize_return exists in typer.rs but operates on ast::FnDef. After: a new ir-shaped specialize_return runs the .11.24.3 typer on a FnIr with parameter Descrs widened/narrowed per call shape. Tests assert that specializing fact(0) yields int_lit(1) (or int_top, depending on widening), and specializing fact(_) yields int_top. The general typer pass continues to use param-Descr Top; specialization is opt-in via an explicit call site.

## Deliverables

src/ir_typer.rs:
- `pub fn specialize_return(m: &Module, fn_id: FnId, params: &[Descr]) -> Descr`.
- Implementation: clone the per-block env machinery from .11.24.3, but seed the entry block's parameter Descrs from the `params` slice instead of widening to Top. Run flow-sensitive narrowing to fixpoint. Read the Descr at every Term::Return / Term::Halt site; union them.
- Recursive call handling: when the body calls itself with narrowed args, fall back to Top to avoid divergence (existing widen mechanism in typer.rs already does this; reuse).
- Public API surface for .13 tier-up: `CompiledModule::compile_specialized(&self, fn_id, params)` — out of scope; stub returns existing CompiledModule for now.

## Tests

- specialize_return on `fn fact(0), do: 1 ; fn fact(n) when n > 0, do: n * fact(n-1)` with params=[int_lit(0)] -> int_lit(1).
- Same fn with params=[int_top] -> int_top.
- specialize_return on `fn id(x), do: x` with params=[atom_lit('ok')] -> atom_lit('ok').
- Recursion termination: a self-recursive fn doesn't loop forever; widening kicks in.

## Out of scope

- Actual code specialization (.13 tier-up's job).
- Polymorphic call-site observation across multi-call programs (.4.4.1 / .4.4.2 reintegration is a separate ticket if needed).

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.24.3.
- BLOCKS: fz-ul4.19.5 (transferable arenas read return-Descr to gauge transferability).
title: specialize_return for IR (sets up tier-up)
